planned_standard: The playbook target is 20-50 accepted tasks after QA and filtering.
rationale: The original pool was treated as candidates and pruned aggressively because many rows were automation-dominated or diagnostically weak.
claim_impact: Blocks locked-benchmark, population-level, and stable subgroup-performance claims.
current_disposition: Report keeps v0.1 as a local research artifact with accepted-count caveats.
required_resolution: Author and hard-review additional T2/T3/T4 candidates until 20-50 accepted rows survive QA.

planned_standard: Accepted tasks should span a meaningful T2/T3/T4 time-horizon ladder.
rationale: The accepted core currently has mostly T2 rows and one T3 row; no T4 stretch row is accepted.
claim_impact: Blocks robust time-horizon scaling claims and weakens bucket-level comparisons.
current_disposition: Report states the current accepted core is a limited T2/T3 slice.
required_resolution: Add independently reviewed T3/T4 tasks, including at least one T4 stretch row, before freeze.

planned_standard: Family and capability summaries should not rely on singleton accepted groups.
rationale: The six accepted tasks intentionally preserve family diversity, but every family is a singleton.
claim_impact: Blocks stable family-level performance means and capability-level generalization.
current_disposition: Report presents family coverage as composition evidence only.
required_resolution: Expand each reported family or avoid family-level estimates until groups have enough accepted tasks.

planned_standard: Human-time estimates should be independently timed or reviewed separately from model runs.
rationale: Author/reviewer p50/p90 estimates exist, but no independent timed solves are committed.
claim_impact: Blocks calibrated time-horizon and time-bucket trend claims.
current_disposition: Report marks human-time values as estimates and provides a timing collection packet.
required_resolution: Collect independent Lean-human timing rows for every accepted task.

planned_standard: Accepted tasks should have non-author task-quality review before benchmark-grade claims.
rationale: The independent review schema and packet are ready, but no real non-author review rows are committed.
claim_impact: Blocks independent task-quality and benchmark-grade accepted-core claims.
current_disposition: Report keeps accepted-core quality as internal review evidence only.
required_resolution: Collect non-author review rows for every accepted_v0 task and rerun review-status audits.

planned_standard: The planned accepted_v0 x scaffold ladder should have exact-k non-infra rows before scaffold-effect claims.
rationale: Only a one-shot smoke cell has non-infra provider evidence; lookup and lookup_unlimited cells are unobserved.
claim_impact: Blocks empirical scaffold-effect, lookup-benefit, and iterative-debug claims.
current_disposition: Report includes only the prospective scaffold plan and strict coverage ledger.
required_resolution: Run exact-k provider sweeps across accepted_v0 x one-shot/lookup/lookup_unlimited cells.

planned_standard: Frontier/open-model claims require documented provider rows across the planned accepted-core sweep.
rationale: Committed non-local rows are smoke provenance only and include too little coverage for model estimates.
claim_impact: Blocks frontier-performance, model-ranking, and benchmark pass-rate claims.
current_disposition: Report uses provider rows as provenance only and keeps performance claims blocked.
required_resolution: Run documented provider sweeps after local and hosted QA stabilize, preserving transcripts and model versions.

planned_standard: Failure-mode distributions should be based on broad failed provider runs with transcript review.
rationale: The codebook, queue, and smoke review workflow exist, but broad failed provider rows are absent.
claim_impact: Blocks dominant-failure-mode and distributional taxonomy claims.
current_disposition: Report presents the workflow and smoke adjudications only.
required_resolution: After broad sweeps, complete independent transcript review before reporting failure distributions.

planned_standard: Recommended performance plots should report raw n and uncertainty only after adequate coverage.
rationale: Performance plots are intentionally omitted because planned provider/scaffold coverage is missing.
claim_impact: Prevents accidental publication of unsupported pass@10, scaffold, family, or bucket performance plots.
current_disposition: Figure manifest and statistical-reporting audit block performance plots while allowing descriptive task-count figures.
required_resolution: Regenerate performance plots only after exact-k provider coverage and accepted-task scale meet the statistical thresholds.

planned_standard: Final benchmark delivery requires hosted QA, Env Linter, finding dispositions, and exact problem-version mappings.
rationale: Taiga packaging scaffolds and local wrapper-isolation checks exist, but no hosted result rows are committed.
claim_impact: Blocks hosted-QA, locked-benchmark, and final problem-version claims.
current_disposition: Report labels hosted readiness as local preparation only.
required_resolution: Run hosted preflight, Full Env QA, Env Linter, disposition findings, and commit immutable version mappings.

planned_standard: Rejected and calibration rows should not enter accepted-core benchmark statistics.
rationale: The repo retains non-core rows for auditability and lower-bound calibration but excludes them from accepted-core claims.
claim_impact: Prevents easy or duplicate tasks from inflating accepted-core quality or performance statistics.
current_disposition: Metadata, candidate-pruning, and report-count audits separate accepted, calibration, and rejected rows.
required_resolution: Maintain status separation whenever tasks are added or downgraded.
